title: feat: add `--format` flag to artifact inspect
body:
Many commands support the `--format` flag which accept a go template to allow for formatting for certain values, but it is not yet implemented for artifact inspect command.

Adding this feature will allow easy formatting in scripts as well as running it on a terminal.

This feature is implemented for artifact inspect by taking reference from images and network commands implementation.

Fixes: [#27112](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27112)

```release-note
Flag `--format` has been added to `podman artifact inspect` command to allow for formatting of output data using go template
```

comments.body: I don't think that is right, it will skip the --format check for all inspect commands if I understand this correctly.
@skyraider256526 looks like you need to rebase.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

Not sure why but I getting lint error for files which I have not modified ```go pkg/systemd/quadlet/quadlet.go:961:47: unused-parameter: parameter 'name' seems to be unused, consider removing or renaming it as _ (revive) func ConvertNetwork(network *parser.UnitFile, name string, unitsInfoMap map[string]*UnitInfo, isUser bool) (*parser.UnitFile, error, error) { ^ pkg/systemd/quadlet/quadlet.go:1050:45: unused-parameter: parameter 'name' seems to be unused, consider removing or renaming it as _ (revive) func ConvertVolume(volume *parser.UnitFile, name string, unitsInfoMap map[string]*UnitInfo, isUser bool) (*parser.UnitFile, error, error) { ^ ``` Should I fix those @Luap99 ?
Can you rebase? CI should be fixed now.
That seems to have fixed things. Validate is complaining that there isn't a test added - would you mind adding one (either to `test/e2e/artifact_test.go` or `test/system/702-artifact-created.bats`?

> That seems to have fixed things. Validate is complaining that there isn't a test added - would you mind adding one (either to `test/e2e/artifact_test.go` or `test/system/702-artifact-created.bats`? @mheon I have added a test case in `test/e2e/artifact_test.go` but it is not showing under `Files Changed` tab and the `Validate source code changes` pipeline is also not passing. I have attached a screenshot of the files changed in the commit
Looks like you're good, but a few existing tests broke.

@skyraider256526 the problem is that you have switched the default type by using  different function call.   Before, artifact inspect returned a *single* libartifact.Artifact.  So it was {artifact} and your new implementation returns a []artifact or libartifact.ArtifactList.  i.e. [{artifact}].

It is easy enough to fix with something like:

```
diff --git a/test/e2e/common_test.go b/test/e2e/common_test.go
index 872f6bd780..be001bedfa 100644
--- a/test/e2e/common_test.go
+++ b/test/e2e/common_test.go
@@ -659,11 +659,12 @@ func (s *PodmanSessionIntegration) InspectImageJSON() []inspect.ImageData {
 
 // InspectArtifactToJSON takes the session output of an artifact inspect and returns json
 func (s *PodmanSessionIntegration) InspectArtifactToJSON() libartifact.Artifact {
-       a := libartifact.Artifact{}
+       var a libartifact.ArtifactList
        inspectOut := s.OutputToString()
        err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(inspectOut), &a)
        Expect(err).ToNot(HaveOccurred())
-       return a
+       foo := a[0]
+       return *foo
 }
```

BUT two major things.

1.  The above code only demonstrates a spot fix.  You should really alter the tests to deal with an ArtifactList being return or some other creative approach.
2. More importantly, the change you suggest will break the current command line and as such cannot be done in Podman 5.  You could propose this for Podman 6, but the team would need to ack this as a good change.  I think you can still accomplish what you wanted by manipulating the return of the the Inspect calls by checking for a valid entry and returning it or an empty {}.  I didn't look deep here.

I personally would revert to original code and change how things are output as a spot fix.

I'm going to loop in others for a broader response as well.  @Honny1 @mheon @ninja-quokka  do you also agree?

There is some precedent for inspect returning a single artifact struct when a single artifact is inspected, and a []Artifact if multiple artifacts are inspected. We did this with pods for a while. It's not good practice, but it should hold us over until Podman 6 when we can change the type. Changing is definitely appropriate, though, especially as `podman inspect` now includes artifact support, and should always return an array of objects.

Correct the output for podman artifact inspect must stay a single object, compare https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/d10e77e1bcd25306ab8afd4ce7da16eed3c67840 for how it is done and we can enable it as part of 6.0 like I did then here https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/35d1f1221390eb1f36f16662c253542f57e135ab
